source_files: experimental/holograph-runtime/adapters/nginx_adapter.py — tails /var/log/nginx/access.log, emits one Thinga per request with {method, path, status, ip, bytes} on request-stream facet + ttl 5s
notes: Streaming kind: each HTTP request becomes a short-lived Thing that lives ~5s then despawns via ttl. Adapter publishes one per access.log line.

tile_color palette: 2xx green (0x44cc44=4509252), 3xx amber (0xe0c200=14732800), 4xx orange (0xffaa00=16753920), 5xx red (0xff4444=16729156), default purple (0x44ad88=4500616). A future request-color-by-status handler reads request-stream.status into tile material.color.
ttl 5s matches the adapter's spawn-time ttl.

fall_speed_per_second 0.3 — for the visual effect of requests drifting downward like rain on the east monitor wall. Today no movement (position.velocity unused on demo spawns); the fall handler lands later.

request-stream facet already exists as a stub at priority 51 in src/ankhor/facets/index.js. This iter doesn't add a tick to it — the streaming visual is what makes the actor pattern attractive but the substrate held shape: spawn + ttl + (future) status-color is enough. Strike 0/3 toward actor-lift remains.
